summary:
the broader picture now.

The cross-cutting signal to watch is the 2-year yield. It is the most sensitive barometer for whether the market believes the Fed can actually stay on hold while the administration calls for emergency cuts. If the 2-year continues to lead the 10-year higher, the pressure on corporate funding costs will eventually break the seal on those tight OAS levels.

the broader picture now.

The broader picture now is one of a "convexity trap." For a systematic credit PM, the "so what" here is the exhaustion of the carry trade. When spreads are at these historical tights, the distribution of outcomes is heavily skewed toward widening. You are essentially picking up pennies in front of a geopolitical steamroller.

the broader picture now.

The broader picture now is the final closing of the "convexity trap" we discussed yesterday. When you are long credit at these tights, your upside is capped by the par call or limited spread compression, while your downside is a cavernous widening if the macro floor gives way.

the broader picture now.

If you are running a systematic book, watch the 2-year yield and the 2s/10s curve slope. If the front end continues to lead the sell-off, the pressure on corporate funding costs will eventually force a re-rating of those tight OAS levels. The carry is attractive until the volatility eat-through destroys your total return.

the broader picture now.

The broader picture now is the final closing of the "convexity trap" we’ve been tracking. For a systematic credit PM, the "so what" here is the total exhaustion of spread compression as a source of return.

the broader picture now.

The broader picture now is the final closing of the "convexity trap" we have been tracking. For a systematic credit manager, the takeaway is the total exhaustion of spread compression as a source of return. We have a situation where equities are pricing in a profit boom while the rates market is pricing in a sustained inflation shock.

the broader picture now.

The broader picture now is the final closing of the "convexity trap" we have been tracking all week. For a systematic portfolio manager, the takeaway is the total exhaustion of spread compression as a source of alpha. We have a situation where equities are pricing in a profit boom while the rates market is pricing in a sustained inflation shock. This tension cannot hold.

the broader picture now.

The broader picture now is the final closing of the "convexity trap" we have been tracking all week. For a systematic portfolio manager, the takeaway is the total exhaustion of spread compression as a source of alpha. We have a situation where equities are pricing in a profit boom while the rates market is pricing in a sustained inflation shock. This tension cannot hold.
